raw_consumer_statement: On XXXX I made an online purchase for XXXX. I started the having second thoughts about it, and as I was looking up the product, I got a fraud alert from the credit card company. I automatically called them and ask them to stop the payment. This was less than XXXX to XXXX minutes after I had made the purchase. They replied that they could not stop the payment because it had already been made. I told them that they had just sent me a fraud alert and that I couldn't believe they had paid it in that short of time. We argued back and forth, till I finally asked to speak to a supervisor. When they finally transferred me XXXX to XXXX minutes later to a supervisor, we argued back and forth about the transaction. I finally told them to just cancel that credit card completely. It was the first time I had ever used it, and I didn't want it anymore. They tried to tell me they would issue me a new card with a new number, but I just told them no I wanted no part of it. Now I've gotten a bill not only for the original payment but for an additional {$10.00} late fee. I have told them I will not pay this.
